card.title: A platform built for slides that needed to learn how to show documents.
card.subtitle: A reader redesign to make SlideShare's 70M+ users actually read.

sections.title: A platform built for slides that needed to learn how to show documents.
sections.subtitle: SlideShare is a global UGC platform with 70M+ users. I redesigned the document reader to improve readability across multiple formats increasing engagement and ad revenue.

meta.team: Product Designer · UX Researcher · Product Manager · Lead Engineer

sections.label: THE PROBLEM
sections.title: It all started with slide presentations.
sections.body: But when it came to documents, users needed a reader that got out of the way: one that let them evaluate and read without distraction. For the product, longer, more focused sessions meant more ad value and a layout that could finally scale across formats.

sections.label: THE APPROACH
sections.title: Adapting the reader for every format.
sections.body: I redefined the hierarchy to support multiple formats while aligning with Scribd's design system, improving orientation in longer documents. I simplified the interface and rebalanced ads and recommendations, introducing adaptive patterns based on document length to keep content central without compromising revenue.

image.alt: SlideShare redesigned reader on a laptop

sections.label: SUCCESS MEASURES
sections.title: The numbers moved in the right direction.
sections.body: Longer reading sessions translated directly into business outcomes. Also ad revenue on desktop grew 7%, driven by users who stayed long enough to see more of the page.

sections.title: Giving Learner Home's navigation the structure it never had.
